questions.question:
A control plane component recovery brief is incomplete and contains unsafe scheduler/controller-manager troubleshooting guidance.

A ConfigMap named `component-repair-brief` already exists in namespace `controlplane-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `schedulerManifest` is `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-scheduler.yaml`
- `controllerManagerManifest` is `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml`
- `schedulerHealthz` is `https://127.0.0.1:10259/healthz`
- `controllerManagerHealthz` is `https://127.0.0.1:10257/healthz`
- `schedulerKubeconfig` is `/etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf`
- `controllerManagerKubeconfig` is `/etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf`
- `schedulerLogHint` is `journalctl -u kubelet | grep kube-scheduler`
- `controllerManagerLogHint` is `journalctl -u kubelet | grep kube-controller-manager`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q501/component-repair-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q501/control-plane-checklist.txt` with the sections `Scheduler`, `Controller Manager`, and `Verification`

Do not restart static Pods and do not replace files under `/etc/kubernetes/manifests` during this drill.

verification.description: The repair brief ConfigMap uses the intended manifest paths, kubeconfig references, health endpoints, and log hints
The exported plain-text checklist contains the required sections and exact scheduler/controller-manager troubleshooting commands
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe recovery guidance is removed

questions.question:
A StatefulSet named `echo-api` and Services `echo-api` and `echo-api-headless` in namespace `connectivity-lab` are not providing the intended in-cluster connectivity.

Fix the workload so that:
- Service `echo-api` stays `ClusterIP`
- Service `echo-api` selects pods with label `app=echo-api`
- Service `echo-api` exposes port `8080` and targets port `8080`
- Service `echo-api-headless` stays headless (`clusterIP: None`)
- Service `echo-api-headless` selects pods with label `app=echo-api`
- StatefulSet `echo-api` keeps `serviceName: echo-api-headless`
- StatefulSet `echo-api` keeps `replicas: 1`
- the StatefulSet becomes Ready
- `net-debug` can fetch `ok` from `http://echo-api:8080/healthz`
- `net-debug` can fetch `ok` from `http://echo-api-0.echo-api-headless.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local:8080/healthz`
- `net-debug` can resolve `echo-api.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local`

Do not delete the StatefulSet, do not create new Pods, and do not convert either Service into a NodePort or LoadBalancer.

verification.description: Services echo-api and echo-api-headless keep the intended selectors and ports while StatefulSet echo-api keeps the expected serviceName and replica count
StatefulSet echo-api becomes Ready and net-debug can resolve the Service name and fetch ok through Service echo-api
net-debug can fetch ok through the headless ordinal DNS name after the connectivity repair

questions.question:
A Service named `echo-api` in namespace `service-debug-lab` is not exposing the intended backend pods.

Fix the Service so that:
- the Service keeps name `echo-api`
- the Service stays `ClusterIP`
- the selector is `app=echo-api`
- the Service exposes port `8080`
- the Service targets port `8080`
- Deployment `echo-api` remains at `replicas: 2`
- Deployment `echo-api` becomes Available
- Service `echo-api` publishes `2` ready endpoints
- `net-debug` can fetch `ok` from `http://echo-api:8080/healthz`

Do not patch the Deployment, do not create Ingress or Gateway API resources, and do not delete the Service.

verification.description: Service echo-api uses the intended type, selector, port, and targetPort contract
Deployment echo-api becomes Available and Service echo-api publishes 2 ready endpoints
net-debug can fetch ok through Service echo-api after the exposure repair
